courses.title: Special Topics in Chicanx and Latinx Studies
Race and Ethnicity in the United States
Introduction to Racial and Ethnic Groups

notes: DATED MATERIAL, SUBJECT TO CHANGE. PLEASE CONSULT CURRENT UCSD GENERAL CATALOG FOR ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION. The Chicanx and Latinx studies major and minor explore the life and culture of Chicanx and Latinx communities in the United States and beyond. This encompasses all Chicanx and Latinx experiences and histories while emphasizing perspectives from the arts, humanities, social sciences, and STEM fields. This includes topics like immigration, borders, policing, race, gender, sexuality, disability, class, public health, culture, indigeneity, social justice, and other issues affecting these communities. Through an interdisciplinary course of study drawing upon course offerings in the Departments of Critical Gender Studies, Communication, Ethnic Studies, History, Linguistics, Literature, Music, Philosophy, Political Science, Theatre and Dance, Urban Studies and Planning, and Visual Arts, among others, students will gain an understanding of this important segment of the US population.

UC San Diego Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) credit policies are detailed in the links below: Advanced Placement (AP) https://www.ucsd.edu/catalog/pdf/APC-chart.pdf International Baccalaureate (IB) https://catalog.ucsd.edu/_files/international-baccalaureate-credits-chart.pdf
